input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS → iOS + Android:

---

## 1. Install Xcode (iOS requirement)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install Xcode **Command Line Tools**: `xcode-select --install`
- Open Xcode once to accept the license and install components
- **Discover version:** MAUI docs list the minimum Xcode version per .NET release at [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/what-is-maui)

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **LTS** or **STS** release
- Use the macOS **Arm64** installer if on Apple Silicon, **x64** for Intel
- **Verify:** `dotnet --version`

## 3. Install the MAUI workload

```bash
dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, and platform dependencies
- **Discover available versions:** `dotnet workload search maui`
- **Verify:** `dotnet workload list` — should show `maui` as installed

## 4. Install JDK (Android requirement)

- The MAUI Android workload bundles a JDK, but if you need one explicitly:
- **Source:** [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) (JDK 17 recommended)
- Set `JAVA_HOME` in your shell profile
- **Verify:** `java --version`

## 5. Android SDK & Emulator

- The `maui-android` workload installs the Android SDK automatically via the .NET build system
- On first build, it will accept licenses and download platform tools
- To manage manually, install **Android Studio** from [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) and use its SDK Manager
- **Verify:** `dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies -f net9.0-android -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true` (adjust TFM to your .NET version)

## 6. iOS Simulator setup

- Comes with Xcode — open **Xcode → Settings → Platforms** to download specific simulator runtimes (e.g., iOS 18)
- **Verify:** `xcrun simctl list devices`

## 7. Create & run a test project

```bash
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# iOS (simulator)
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-ios

# Android (emulator — start one first via Android Studio)
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-android
```

## 8. Verification checklist

| Check | Command |
|---|---|
| .NET SDK | `dotnet --info` |
| MAUI workload | `dotnet workload list` |
| Xcode CLI tools | `xcode-select -p` |
| iOS simulators | `xcrun simctl list devices available` |
| Java | `java --version` |
| Android SDK | `dotnet build` an Android target successfully |

## Key tips

- **Version discovery:** Always check the [.NET MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) for the compatibility matrix (which .NET SDK ↔ Xcode ↔ Android API level).
- **Keep workloads updated:** `dotnet workload update`
- **VS Code:** Install the **.NET MAUI Extension** and **C# Dev Kit** for IDE support if not using Visual Studio for Mac's successor (VS Code is now the recommended macOS IDE).
